jokes.text: David? / David, what is this?! / It looks like a bike. / Is it yours? / Yes. Yes, it is.
jokes.setup: Someone discovers an unexpected bike and demands explanation
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact confirmation of the obvious
jokes.explanation: David's deadpan delivery of the obvious creates mild humor through understatement

jokes.text: My God, imagine what kind of ring he'd buy! Like an opal, or something. Ooh, like a pear-shaped opal. (Groans)
jokes.setup: Alexis catastrophizing about Mutt's gift-giving abilities
jokes.punchline: The specific horror of a pear-shaped opal engagement ring
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific vanity and fear, with escalating specificity that makes it funnier

jokes.text: Well, you said you missed spin class, so... I did! I did say that I missed spin class. And so you bought me a bike! Because I said that I missed spin class. Because you're so thoughtful and perceptive.
jokes.setup: Mutt's well-meaning but misguided gift logic
jokes.punchline: Alexis's forced enthusiasm revealing the disconnect
jokes.explanation: The repetitive structure emphasizes the awkwardness of the mismatch between gift and recipient

jokes.text: (Whispering) I hate you, David!
jokes.setup: Alexis forced into an uncomfortable situation with Mutt
jokes.punchline: Her whispered blame to David while maintaining fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Quick, sharp sibling moment that contrasts with her public performance

jokes.text: John, do you think they did this with our things? No, not ours, no. They put our stuff right on the lawn.
jokes.setup: Moira wondering if their belongings got the estate sale treatment
jokes.punchline: Johnny's correction that their stuff got even worse treatment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their financial downfall with perfect character-specific self-awareness

jokes.text: Look at this poor woman's life strewn across cheap folding tables.
jokes.setup: The Roses are at an estate sale looking at used items
jokes.punchline: Moira's melodramatic description of the mundane yard sale setup
jokes.explanation: Pure Moira - turning a yard sale into a tragic tableau with her theatrical language

jokes.text: Well, except they'd have been saying, 'what great taste they had!'
jokes.setup: Discussion of strangers judging people through their possessions
jokes.punchline: Moira's unshakeable confidence in her superior taste
jokes.explanation: Pure Moira vanity perfectly delivered - she can't imagine anyone criticizing her taste

jokes.text: So was my Galapagonian tortoise-shell foot bath. And now some lonely hoarder is letting his cats poop in it.
jokes.setup: Moira lamenting her lost luxury items
jokes.punchline: The specific, grotesque fate of her exotic foot bath
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific - combines Moira's extravagant past with vivid, disgusting imagery

jokes.text: And you know whose name came to mind? I don't-i don't know, Rachel Zoe? Yours!
jokes.setup: Roland's buildup about who he trusts with fashion advice
jokes.punchline: David's reasonable guess vs. Roland actually picking him
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection with David's fashion-world reference before the reveal

jokes.text: You're just describing what shopping is.
jokes.setup: Roland elaborately explaining the shopping process
jokes.punchline: David's dry observation about the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and deadpan delivery cuts through Roland's unnecessary explanation

jokes.text: And who knows elegance better than you, pal? I mean, you're the only elegant guy I know!
jokes.setup: Roland flattering David about his fashion sense
jokes.punchline: The implication that Roland's social circle is very limited
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the unintended insult about Roland's limited social circle

jokes.text: I don't know how to ride a bike. What? I said, I don't know how to ride a bike!
jokes.setup: Alexis making excuses about not wanting to ride the bike
jokes.punchline: The revelation that she literally can't ride a bike
jokes.explanation: Perfect revelation that explains her earlier behavior and fits her privileged character

jokes.text: Poor dear, enchanted by a death bed.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn excited about finding a mattress at an estate sale
jokes.punchline: Moira's morbid characterization of the mattress
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira wit - dark, eloquent, and perfectly dismissive of others' enthusiasm

jokes.text: Okay, these are pants that just so happen to have a pant fabric over the front and back... Okay. Yeah, it's a skirt.
jokes.setup: David trying to defend his fashion choice to Roland
jokes.punchline: His immediate capitulation when pressed
jokes.explanation: David's elaborate explanation followed by immediate defeat shows his vulnerability

jokes.text: Okay, these are pants that just so happen to have a pant fabric over the front and back... Okay. Yeah, it's a skirt.
jokes.setup: Roland commenting on David's clothing choice
jokes.punchline: David's elaborate defense followed by his defeated admission
jokes.explanation: David's defensive explanation followed by defeat is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Jocelyn and I like to have a little fun with that idea, if you know what I mean. She plays Meryl, and I play the nervous but ambitious intern who's new to the city.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining his Devil Wears Prada roleplay
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable specificity of their bedroom games
jokes.explanation: Hilariously uncomfortable overshare that perfectly captures Roland's character

jokes.text: So you're the Anne Hathaway in that situation? I don't know who that is.
jokes.setup: David trying to process Roland's roleplay confession
jokes.punchline: Roland's oblivious response to the main actress reference
jokes.explanation: Roland's cultural ignorance adds to the absurdity of his elaborate roleplay

jokes.text: And David, was such a little drama queen when my parents tried teaching him, that they threw all the bikes into this big bonfire up in the Hamptons, and we never talked about it again.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why neither sibling learned to ride bikes
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously extreme wealthy family solution to a minor problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect character backstory that's both absurd and completely believable for this family

jokes.text: Yeah well, most kids also get head lice. I'm sorry if the opportunity never presented itself.
jokes.setup: Mutt says most kids learn to ride bikes
jokes.punchline: Alexis's defensive comparison to head lice and sarcastic apology
jokes.explanation: Alexis's privileged defensiveness - equating normal childhood with parasites

jokes.text: And try getting into 'Kiss Kiss' in Tokyo without a lock of human hair.
jokes.setup: Alexis listing her unusual life skills
jokes.punchline: The bizarre entry requirement for an exclusive club
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and weird detail about Alexis's exotic past experiences

jokes.text: Well, because I see a couple of 'Lost Boys.'
jokes.setup: Wendy approaching two men looking confused in a women's store
jokes.punchline: The Peter Pan reference to their lost appearance
jokes.explanation: Generic pun that any sales associate might make - not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, I'm actually in really good hands, because this man has a woman's touch. I don't... I don't know what that means.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to compliment David's fashion sense
jokes.punchline: David's uncomfortable confusion at the phrasing
jokes.explanation: Roland's awkward compliment and David's genuine confusion create mild tension

jokes.text: Well, this is a real 'Sophie's Choice!' I hope that you and Jocelyn don't role play that at home.
jokes.setup: Roland comparing two blouses to Sophie's Choice
jokes.punchline: David's horror at the roleplay implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that escalates the roleplay concept to darker territory

jokes.text: Hell, we've played every Streep movie.
jokes.setup: David's suggestion about Sophie's Choice roleplay
jokes.punchline: Roland's enthusiastic confirmation that they've done them all
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that implies increasingly inappropriate scenarios

jokes.text: Skanky! I think the clothes are a bit skanky.
jokes.setup: David struggling to find a diplomatic way to criticize the clothes
jokes.punchline: His eventual blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: David's filter finally breaking down leads to honest but tactless assessment

jokes.text: Which uh, I hope you can afford. You know, considering your financial situation.
jokes.setup: Ray announcing Johnny won the cufflinks for $15
jokes.punchline: The unnecessary dig about their poverty
jokes.explanation: Ray's tactless comment about their financial situation creates awkward humor

jokes.text: We are currently sleeping on a rectangular collection of knives and barbed wire, John.
jokes.setup: Johnny questions Moira's aggressive bidding on the mattress
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic description of their current motel mattress
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira hyperbole - turning an uncomfortable mattress into medieval torture

jokes.text: (Bleep!)
jokes.setup: Johnny losing the mattress bidding war to Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: His censored outburst of frustration
jokes.explanation: Standard frustrated reaction beat - the censoring adds mild humor

jokes.text: God, you're like a fancy dinner plate that you don't wanna nudge off the edge of the table.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to describe David's delicate nature
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and accurate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly poetic metaphor from Roland that actually captures David perfectly

jokes.text: Um listen, I don't have a lot to my name right now, but I do have one thing. Self-respect? No, taste.
jokes.setup: David about to reveal what he values most
jokes.punchline: Roland's reasonable guess vs. David's actual priority
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David values taste over self-respect, delivered with great structure

jokes.text: People were saying that a girl was struggling with her bike. I kinda just assumed it was a toddler.
jokes.setup: Twyla approaching to help with the bike situation
jokes.punchline: Her assumption about who would need bike help
jokes.explanation: Twyla's innocent delivery makes the age comparison funnier

jokes.text: Get the hell outta here, you dumb broad! (Chuckles) That was another thing he used to say.
jokes.setup: Twyla sharing her mom's boyfriend's bike teaching method
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation of his verbal abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Twyla's characteristic casual acceptance of dysfunction

jokes.text: Yeah, not enough bounce for the bumps, if you know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Roland explaining why they don't want the mattress
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland overshare - uncomfortable but predictable sexual reference

jokes.text: Oh God, that's memory foam, John! ... The Kennedys aren't Roland! The Kennedys have regular medicals, daily laundering!
jokes.setup: Moira realizing they're sleeping on Roland's used mattress
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly specific reasons why the Kennedy comparison fails
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira escalation with specific elite standards contrasted against Roland

jokes.text: Oh my God, you know that I have bad foot-eye coordination, you didn't have to wear corrective leg braces for three months.
jokes.setup: David protesting about learning to ride a bike
jokes.punchline: The revelation of his leg brace period
jokes.explanation: Another perfect character detail about David's physically fragile past

jokes.text: Oh my God, you know that I have bad foot-eye coordination, you didn't have to wear corrective leg braces for three months.
jokes.setup: David protesting about learning to ride a bike
jokes.punchline: His claim about foot-eye coordination and leg braces
jokes.explanation: David's dramatic backstory and made-up coordination issues

jokes.text: Honey, I need to ask you a serious question, has it gotten worse or are you just not wearing any makeup?
jokes.setup: Moira approaching sleeping Alexis for a serious conversation
jokes.punchline: The 'serious question' being about Alexis's appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - her idea of maternal concern is purely aesthetic
